36.2: “Surround unto me a little, and I will show you, that there are words yet to God.
36.3: I will lift up my knowledge from afar, and to my Maker I will ascribe righteousness.
36.4: For truly, my words are not false; the one complete in knowledge is with you.
36.5: Behold, God is mighty, and He does not despise; He is mighty in strength of heart.
36.6: He does not keep the wicked alive, and He gives justice to the afflicted.
36.7: He does not take away His eyes from the righteous, but they are with kings on the throne; and He has seated them forever, and they are exalted.
36.8: And if they are bound in the fetters, caught in the cords of affliction,
36.9: then he declares to them their work and their transgressions, because they magnified themselves.
36.10: And He opens their ear to correction, and He says that they should turn back from iniquity.
36.11: If they do hear and serve, they will complete their days in good, and their years in pleasantness.
36.12: And if they do not obey, by a weapon they shall pass away, and they shall die as without knowledge.
36.13: And the godless of heart set the nose; they do not cry out when He binds them.
36.14: Their soul dies in youth, and their life among the male shrine prostitutes.
36.15: He rescues the afflicted one by his affliction, and uncovers their ear by oppression.
36.16: And indeed He has drawn you from the mouth of distress, into a broad place without constraint under it, and the resting of your table is full of richness.
36.17: And you are filled with the judgment of the wicked; judgment and justice take hold—
36.18: for wrath, lest it entice you with mocking, and a large bribe not let it stretch you.
36.19: Can your opulence make arrangement in time of distress, or all the forces of strength?
36.20: Do not long for the night, for the coming up of peoples in place of them.
36.21: Take heed not to turn to iniquity, for upon this you have been chosen by affliction.
36.22: Behold, God is exalted in His power; who is a teacher like Him?
36.23: Who has appointed for Him His way? Or who has said, ‘You have done unrighteousness’?
36.24: Remember that you should magnify His work, of which men have sung.
36.25: Every man has gazed on it; mankind looks on it from afar.
36.26: Behold, God is great, and we do not know Him , and there is no searching the number of His years.
36.27: For He draws up drops of water; they distill as rain from the mist,
36.28: which the clouds pour down; they drop upon man in abundance.
36.29: Indeed, if one will understand the spreading out of a cloud, the noises from His pavilion?
36.30: Behold, He scatters His lightning around Him, and covers the depths of the sea.
36.31: For by these He judges the peoples; He gives food in abundance.
36.32: With His palms He covers the lightning, and He gives a charge upon it for its striking.
36.33: By its shouting He declares concerning it, the cattle also concerning the arising.

37.1: “ Indeed at this my heart trembles, and it leaps from its place.
37.2: Listen closely to the quaking of His voice, and the rumbling from His mouth that comes forth.
37.3: Beneath all the heaven He unleashes it, and His lightning is over the edges of the earth.
37.4: After it, a voice roars; He thunders with the voice of His majesty, and He does not restrain them when His voice is heard.
37.5: God thunders with His voice wondrously; He does great things that we cannot know.
37.6: For to the snow He says, ‘Fall on the earth,’ and to the shower of rain, and to the shower, ‘ Be the rains of His might.’
37.7: He seals up the hand of every man, for the knowing of His work by all men.
37.8: And the beast comes into its lair, and in its dens it shall dwell.
37.9: From its chamber the whirlwind comes, and the cold from the scattering winds.
37.10: From the breath of God, ice is given, and the breadth of the waters is in constraint.
37.11: Also with moisture He loads the thick cloud; His lightning scatters the cloud.
37.12: And it turns around, being turned about by His guidance, to do all of what He commands them above the face of the habitation of the earth.
37.13: If for the rod, if for His land, if in His loving devotion, He makes it find it.
37.14: Give ear to this, O Job; stand and consider the wonders of God.
37.15: Do you know how God does place upon them, and how He causes the light of His cloud to shine?
37.16: Do you know about the balancing of the cloud, the wonders of the One perfect in knowledge,
37.17: you whose garments are hot in the quieting of the earth from the south?
37.18: Have you beaten the skies with him, strong as a mirror of cast metal?
37.19: Make us know what we should say to Him; we cannot arrange from the face of the darkness.
37.20: Should it be told to Him that I would speak, If a man spoke that he would be swallowed up?
37.21: And now they cannot look at the light; it is bright in the clouds when the wind has passed over and cleansed them.
37.22: Gold comes from the north; upon God is a fearsome majesty.
37.23: Shaddai—we cannot find Him out! He is great in power and judgment, and abundant in righteousness, He does not oppress.
37.24: Therefore men fear Him; He does not regard any of the wise of heart.”

38.1: And YHWH answered Job out of the whirlwind, and He said:
38.2: “Who is this darkening counsel with words lacking knowledge?
38.3: Gird up your loins now like a man, and I will question you, and you shall answer Me.
38.4: Where were you at my founding of the earth? Declare it if you know with understanding.
38.5: Who set its measurements? Surely you must know! Or who stretched over it a line?
38.6: On what were its bases made to sink in , or who cast the stone of its corner,
38.7: at the singing together by the stars of the morning, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
38.8: Or who shut in the sea with doors, at its coming forth in its bursting from the womb,
38.9: in my setting for the cloud its garment, and for the dark cloud its swaddling band,
38.10: when I broke forth over it My decree, and set in place a bar and doors,
38.11: and I said, ‘Up to here you may come, and you shall not go further; and here shall be set the pride of your waves’?
38.12: have you commanded the morning from your days, and made the dawn to know its place,
38.13: for it to take hold of the edges of the earth, and the wicked be shaken out of it?
38.14: It is turned over like clay under a seal, and they stand out like a garment.
38.15: And their light is withheld from the wicked, and the arm upraised is broken.
38.16: Have you come to the springs of the sea, or in search of the depths have you walked about?
38.17: Have the gates of death been uncovered to you, or have you seen the doors of the shadow of death?
38.18: Have you comprehended to the breadths of the earth? Declare it if you know all of it.
38.19: Where is this way to the dwelling of light? And of darkness, where is this its place,
38.20: that you may take it to its border, that you may know the paths to its home?
38.21: You must know it , for you were born then, and the number of your days is great!
38.22: Have you come to the treasury of snow, or have you seen the treasury of hail,
38.23: that I have held back for a time of trouble, for a day of battle and war?
38.24: Where is this, the way light is apportioned? The east wind is scattered over the earth.
38.25: Who has divided out a channel for the flood, or a path for the lightning of the thunderings,
38.26: to make rain come upon a land without man, a desert without man in it,
38.27: to satisfy the waste and the desolation, and to make sprout the shoot of the grass?
38.28: Is there a father for the rain? Or who has begotten the drops of dew?
38.29: From whose womb comes forth the ice? And the frost of the heavens, who gives birth to it?
38.30: Like stone, the waters hide themselves, and the face of the deep is seized.
38.31: Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades, or can you open the cords of Orion?
38.32: Can you bring forth Mazzaroth in its time? Or can you lead out the Bear with her cubs?
38.33: Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Or can you set its rule over the earth?
38.34: Can you raise to the cloud your voice, and an abundance of waters will cover you?
38.35: Can you send out lightnings, that they may go and say to you, ‘Behold us’?
38.36: Who has put wisdom in the inner parts? Or who has given to the heart understanding?
38.37: Who can count the clouds by wisdom? Or the water skins of the heavens, who can tilt,
38.38: in the pouring of the dust into a mass, and the clods clinging together?
38.39: Can you hunt prey for a lion, or satisfy the life of young lions,
38.40: when they crouch in the dens, dwelling in a thicket, lying in wait in it ?
38.41: Who provides to the raven its food when its children cry out to God and wander for lack of food?

39.1: “ Do you know the time of the bearing of the goats of the rock? Can you observe the twisting of the does?
39.2: Can you number the months they fulfill? Or do you know the time of their giving birth?
39.3: They bend down and squeeze out their young; their labor pains they send forth.
39.4: Their young ones are healthy; they increase in the field; they go forth and do not return to them.
39.5: Who sent away the wild donkey free? And who opened the bonds of the swift donkey,
39.6: whose I have made the Arabah house, the salt land as his dwelling?
39.7: He laughs at the tumult of the city; the shouts of the driver he does not hear.
39.8: He searches out the mountains for his pasture, and after every green thing he searches.
39.9: Is the wild ox willing to serve you, or will he lodge by your manger?
39.10: Can you bind the wild ox in the furrow with a cord, or will he harrow the valleys after you?
39.11: Will you trust in him because his strength is great, and will you leave to him your labor?
39.12: Will you trust in him that he will bring back your grain and gather it to your threshing floor?
39.13: The wings of the ostrich rejoice, but are not the pinions of a stork and its feathers.
39.14: For she leaves her eggs on the ground, and upon the dust she warms them ,
39.15: and she forgets that a foot may crush it, or that a creature of the field might tread over it.
39.16: Her young she treats harshly as though not unto her; her labor is in vain, without dread;
39.17: for God made her forget wisdom, and He has not given a portion to her in understanding.
39.18: At the time she lifts herself up on high, she laughs at the horse and at its rider.
39.19: Have you given the horse strength? Did you clothe his neck with a mane?
39.20: Can you cause him to rush like a locust? The majesty of his snorting is terror.
39.21: They dig in the valley, and he rejoices in strength; he goes forth to meet the weaponry.
39.22: He laughs at dread and is not dismayed; he does not turn back from the face of the sword.
39.23: Upon him rattles the quiver, the flame of the spear and lance.
39.24: With quaking and raging he swallows the ground; he cannot stand still because of the voice of the shofar.
39.25: In the abundance of the shofar, he says, ‘Aha!’ And from afar He smells the battle, the thunder of captains and shouting.
39.26: By your understanding does the hawk soar and spread his wings toward the south?
39.27: Is it by your mouth that the eagle mounts up and makes his nest on high?
39.28: In the rock he dwells and lodges and his stronghold is upon the tooth of the rock.
39.29: From there he searches out food; from afar his eyes observe.
39.30: And his young ones suck up blood; and where the slain are , there he is .”

40.1: And YHWH answered Job and said,
40.2: “Is a reprover to contend with Shaddai? One rebuking God, let him answer it.”
40.3: And Job answered YHWH and said,
40.4: “Behold, I am slight. How can I reply to You? My hand I place over my mouth.
40.5: Once I have spoken, and I will not answer—and twice, and I will not add.”
40.6: And YHWH answered Job from the whirlwind, and He said,
40.7: “Gird up now your loins like a man; I will question you, and you shall let Me know.
40.8: Would you even break My judgment? Would you consider Me wicked so that you may be proved right?
40.9: Is there an arm like God’s unto you? Or with a voice like His can you thunder?
40.10: Adorn yourself, please, with majesty and loftiness, and with honor and glory clothe yourself.
40.11: Scatter the fury of your nose, and look on every proud one, and bring him low.
40.12: Look on every proud one, and humble him; and tread down the wicked in their place.
40.13: Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in the hidden place.
40.14: And then I will confess to you that your right hand can bring salvation to you.
40.15: Behold, please, Behemoth, which I made with you; he eats grass like the ox.
40.16: Behold, please, his strength is in his loins, and his power is in the muscles of his belly.
40.17: He bends his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are tightly knit.
40.18: His bones are tubes of bronze; his limbs are like bars of iron.
40.19: He is the first of the ways of God; his Maker can bring near His sword.
40.20: For the mountains carry produce to him, and all the creatures of the field play there.
40.21: Beneath the lotus trees he lies down, in the covering of reed and marsh.
40.22: The lotus trees cover him with their shade; the willows of the brook surround him.
40.23: Though the river may press, he does not hurry off ; he is secure, though the Jordan should burst forth toward his mouth.
40.24: Can one take him by his eyes, or pierce his nose with snares?

41.1: “ Can you draw out Leviathan with a hook, and press down his tongue with a rope?
41.2: Can you put a cord in his nose, and with a hook pierce his jaw?
41.3: Will he multiply to you supplications? Indeed, will he speak to you softly?
41.4: Will he cut a covenant with you, to take him as a slave forever?
41.5: Will you play with him like a bird, and leash him for your maidens?
41.6: Will traders barter for him? Will they divide him among the merchants?
41.7: Can you fill his skin with harpoons, or his head with spears of fishing?
41.8: Put your hands on him; may you remember the battle, and not add.
41.9: Behold, the hope of him is false. One is even cast down at the sight of him.
41.10: None is so fierce that he would rouse him; and who is he who will stand before my face?
41.11: Who has brought before Me that I should repay him ? Under all the heavens it is unto Me.
41.12: I will not keep silent about his limbs, and the matter of his strength and the grace of his form.
41.13: Who can uncover the face of his clothing? Who can enter into the double of his bridle?
41.14: The doors of his face, who can open them ? All around his teeth are terror.
41.15: His rows of shields are his pride, shut up tight with a seal.
41.16: One by one they draw near, and air cannot come between them.
41.17: They are joined a man to his brother; they clasp together and cannot be separated.
41.18: His sneezings flash forth light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the dawn.
41.19: From his mouth torches go forth; sparks of fire leap out.
41.20: From his nostrils smoke comes forth, boiling like a pot and a reed.
41.21: His breath will kindle the coals, and a flame goes forth from his mouth.
41.22: In his neck lodges strength, and before him terror dances.
41.23: The folds of his flesh are joined together, c on him, and it annot be shaken.
41.24: His heart is as firm as a stone, even as firm as a lower millstone.
41.25: From his rising up the mighty are afraid; before his thrashing they cleanse themselves from sin.
41.26: The sword reaching him shall not stand— not spear, dart, or lance.
41.27: He regards iron as straw, and bronze as rotten wood.
41.28: The son of the bow cannot make him flee; the stones of the sling are turned over before him like stubble.
41.29: A club is regarded as stubble, and he laughs at the rattle of a lance.
41.30: Beneath him are points of a shard; he spreads out like a threshing sledge upon the mud.
41.31: He makes the deep boil like a pot; he makes the sea like an ointment pot.
41.32: Behind him he makes a path shine; one would think the deep were white-haired.
41.33: There is nothing on earth like him, the one made devoid of fear.
41.34: He beholds every high thing ; he is king over all the sons of pride.”

42.1: And Job answered YHWH and said:
42.2: “I know that You are able to do all things, and a plan from You cannot be thwarted.
42.3: ‘Who is this, concealing counsel without knowledge?’ So I have declared, and did not understand—things too wonderful for me, and I did not know.
42.4: ‘Listen now, and I will speak. I will question you, and you shall inform Me.’
42.5: By the hearing of the ear I had heard of You, and now my eye has seen You.
42.6: Upon thus I despise it , and I repent in dust and ashes.”
42.7: And it came to pass, after YHWH had spoken these words to Job, that YHWH said to Eliphaz the Temanite, “My anger is kindled against you, and against the two friends of yours, for you have? not spoken of Me accurately as My servant Job has .
42.8: And now, take for yourselves seven bulls and seven rams, and go to My servant Job, and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And Job My servant shall pray for you? (for surely I will lift his face), so as not to deal with your? folly, for you? have not spoken of Me accurately, as My servant Job has .”
42.9: Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite went and did according to what YHWH had spoken to them; and YHWH lifted up the face of Job.
42.10: And YHWH turned back the captivity of Job, in his praying for his friends; and YHWH added to all that was unto Job—to double.
42.11: All his brothers, and all his sisters, and all the faces of those knowing him came to him, and they ate bread with him in his house, and they nodded to him and comforted him over all the adversity that YHWH had brought upon him. And each man gave to him one kesitah, and each man one ring of gold.
42.12: And YHWH blessed the latter of Job more than his first, and there were unto him fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand female donkeys.
42.13: And there were unto him seven sons and three daughters.
42.14: And he called the name of the first Jemimah, and the name of the second Keziah, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
42.15: And there were not found women as beautiful as the daughters of Job in all the land; and their father gave to them an inheritance in the midst of their brothers.
42.16: And Job lived after this a hundred and forty years, and he saw his sons and the sons of his sons, four generations.
42.17: And Job died, old and full of days.
